load_notes: Accountability owner and boundary/exclusion holder; should not absorb narrow mature delegated responsibilities.
Highest boundary/delivery load; final assembler-side handoff actor; owns flags and detailed feedback report production.
Writing-side runtime load; owns writing waiting list and participates in envelope progression and queue-container identifier discipline.
Delivery-side runtime load; owns delivery waiting list and supports delivery completion context, requalification, and dispatcher feedback.
Highest queue-bundle orchestration load; realizes operational directives and preserves queue-container terminology at bundle level.
Highest runtime-state load; owns one queue-container moderation, slots, occupancy, lifecycle, and requalification semantics.
Schema/directive-governed preparation component; explicitly excluded from policy hosting and EN-FAM-002.
Mandatory mature timestamp component; participates in all accepted ingress families and is policy-consuming for timestamp mechanism semantics.
Validating-path-only component; policy-consuming for validation semantics but prohibited from hot-path externalized validation.
Policy intake/assignment mediator; does not execute mechanisms and does not replace host components.
Client-side composed contract object; produces/prepares Log Level Envelope but does not replace Assembler Ingress Port.
Server-side receiving/admission port; receives already assembled Log Level Envelope and admits it into queue-container slot.
Administrative/control ingress port; separates policy, metadata, and operational directives from consuming content ingress.
Outbound registry-facing contract surface; must not replace Dispatcher as actor or own registry internals.
External consuming counterpart for typed content ingress and Envelope Assembler integration.
External governance/control source; does not carry internal delegated responsibilities.
External policy source; supplies policy material but does not assign directly to components.
External metadata authority source.
External operational directive source; directives are realized internally by Queue Bundle Agent.
External receiving subsystem; owns lifecycle after valid handoff but is not assembler-owned.
Explicitly excluded read/query world.
Optional future receiver of detailed dispatcher feedback reports.
Classification-only layer; must not appear as primary delegated entity.
